name: Top One Futures
description_short: Top One Futures is a Wyoming-based futures prop firm offering Elite Access, Instant Sim Funded, and S2F Sim PRO accounts from $25K to $150K with a 90% profit split, EOD drawdown, and sub-24-hour payouts.
description_long:
## The $19 Challenge Question That Defines This Firm

Top One Futures wants futures traders to make one specific bet: that a Wyoming prop firm launched in 2024 can deliver sub-24-hour payouts on a 90% split at a lower entry cost than Topstep, Apex, or My Funded Futures. The pitch works if the promo pricing holds and payouts keep landing on time. It fails if you assumed "Elite Access at $19" was the standing rate rather than a rotating discount that requires buying two accounts under code ACCESS. This prop firm review focuses on what you're actually locking into when you swipe a card for that first challenge, because the marketing surface and the underlying account terms are not the same document.

## Reading The Promo Layer Before The Product Layer

Open toponefutures.com and the first thing you see is a rotating banner: 55% off first five accounts for new traders, Elite Access two-for-$19 with code ACCESS, 50% off Elite Daily with code SUMMER, and a countdown timer threatening that Elite Daily is being removed Sunday night. None of these are the base price. The base Elite Access $25K challenge is $39. The base Instant Sim Funded $50K is $340. The two-for-$19 offer means $38 total for two Elite Access $25K accounts, not one account at $19, and it is a promotional code that can be pulled or changed without notice.

This is the first real decision point with Top One Futures. If you are the kind of buyer who reads coupon codes carefully and checks whether the discount applies to activation fees, resets, or only the initial evaluation, the stack of promos genuinely lowers your all-in cost. If you assume the banner price is the price, you will misjudge your monthly outlay by 40 to 60 percent once you factor in resets, activation fees on funded accounts, and the fact that Elite Access is a one-day-pass challenge rather than a no-evaluation instant product.

## The Four Product Tracks And Why They Are Not Interchangeable

Top One Futures runs four distinct account types and the differences matter more than the marketing suggests. Elite Challenge and Elite Daily are evaluation-style products with lower up-front cost and a pass requirement. Elite Access is the flagship one-day-pass challenge starting at $39 for the $25K size, scaling to $63 ($50K), $125 ($100K), and $185 ($150K), with unlimited $35 resets and an activation fee only after passing. Instant Sim Funded skips evaluation entirely at $340 for $50K or $411 for $100K as a one-time fee. S2F Sim PRO is marketed as the highest-payout track, and IGNITE Instant Funding sits alongside the Elite Daily promo stack.

Elite Access has no consistency rule on the challenge phase. Instant Sim Funded has no evaluation but a much larger up-front check. If you plan to trade a single account and scale profits, Instant Sim Funded is the cleaner math. If you plan to buy multiple small accounts and run them as a portfolio, Elite Access with the two-for-$38 promo is the cheaper on-ramp. The Top One Futures rule set differs across these tracks in ways the front page compresses into a single "90% split" claim, and you should read the exact account page for the product you're buying rather than the summary tiles.

## What EOD Drawdown Actually Changes About Your Trading

Every Top One Futures product defaults to end-of-day trailing drawdown, with an intraday option available. This is a meaningful difference from firms that lock you into intraday-only trailing. With EOD, unrealized profit during the session does not tighten your drawdown until the daily close, meaning you can hold a winner into the afternoon without watching the trailing stop crawl up in real time. For scalpers who take small profits and give some back, EOD is materially more forgiving. For traders who already run tight intraday risk and rarely give back open profit, the difference is smaller.

On the $25K Elite Access, the daily loss limit is $625 and trailing max drawdown is $1,000, with a $1,500 profit target and a 1 mini (10 micros) contract cap during the challenge. These numbers are not generous — the ratio of drawdown to target is standard for the segment — but the EOD mode makes hitting them feel different than on Topstep-style intraday trailing. If you have blown accounts specifically because intraday trailing tightened during a lunch pullback, that is the exact failure mode Top One Futures's default setup is designed to remove.

## The Payout Story: 90 Percent, Sub-24 Hours, And What Reduces After Withdrawal Three

Top One Futures markets a 90% profit split, sub-24-hour payout processing, and progressive payout thresholds that drop to 4% after your third withdrawal. The firm claims over $26 million in payouts and 24/7 trader support. News trading is allowed across every product, including FOMC, CPI, and NFP releases — a hard differentiator against firms that either ban news trading outright or restrict position size during red-folder events.

The unverifiable parts: I cannot independently confirm payout timing or the aggregate payout figure without third-party data. Trustpilot and community forums typically move on those claims within a few weeks of a promo cycle, and any buyer should sanity-check current sentiment before committing to a $340 Instant Sim Funded account. The Top One Futures support response time (advertised under a minute on live chat) is easy to test yourself before purchase — open a chat, ask a specific rules question, and see how the answer lines up with the account terms.

## Where Top One Futures Is Structurally Weaker Than The Incumbents

Top One Futures launched in 2024. Topstep has been running futures evaluations since 2012, Apex since 2018, FTMO (though CFD-focused) since 2015. Operating history matters for prop firms specifically because the failure mode is not slow decline — it is a sudden change to payout terms, contract limits, or scaling rules when the firm's economics shift. A firm two years old has not been tested through a full cycle of trader payout volatility.

The platform footprint is also narrower. Top One Futures supports Tradovate, NinjaTrader, and TradingView. Rithmic-only shops, Quantower users, and MotiveWave traders will not find their setup here. There is no proprietary mobile app — mobile trading routes through the underlying platform vendor. If your workflow depends on a firm-branded app for position monitoring away from your desk, that is a gap.

Finally, the promo density is a real cost. Rotating codes make it hard to compare Top One Futures against Tradeify, MyFundedFutures, or Apex on a like-for-like basis. You have to price the current promo, the reset fee ($35 on Elite Access), the activation fee where applicable, and factor in that the promo you bought under may not exist for account number two.

## Who Should Actually Buy This And Who Should Wait

Top One Futures is a fit for a specific buyer: a futures day trader who already has a defined edge, wants EOD drawdown flexibility, needs to trade news events without artificial position restrictions, and can extract the two-for-$38 Elite Access promo cleanly. The affiliate program (10-20% tiered plus 5% second-tier) is aggressive enough to matter for anyone with an audience.

Who should wait: traders who need a firm with five-plus years of payout history before committing capital, traders whose platform of choice sits outside Tradovate/NinjaTrader/TradingView, and traders who want a single stable price sheet without decoding promo codes each cycle. Compared to Topstep, Apex Trader Funding, My Funded Futures, and Tradeify, Top One Futures competes on price and speed rather than on institutional depth. That trade is worth taking for some traders and not for others — the deciding factor is how much you weight two years of operating history against a lower entry cost and faster payout processing.
affiliate_commission: 10–20% tiered (Bronze 10% → Diamond 20%) + 5% second-tier override. Recurring on subscriptions, resets, and activation fees.

EOD drawdown default with intraday option lets traders match risk mode to style
Elite Access two-for-$38 promo delivers one of the lowest entry costs in the segment
Aggressive affiliate program with 10-20% tiered rates plus 5% second-tier commissions

Rotating promo codes (ACCESS, SUMMER, 55% off first 5) obscure true standing pricing

Narrower platform footprint than competitors (three platforms vs five-plus at rivals)

company_background: Top One Futures is a futures-only prop firm headquartered in Cheyenne, Wyoming, launched in 2024. The firm operates four account tracks (Elite Challenge, Elite Daily, Elite Access, Instant Sim Funded, S2F Sim PRO, IGNITE Instant Funding) across Tradovate, NinjaTrader, and TradingView, and markets over $26 million in cumulative payouts with 24/7 trader support.
